prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Refine gating tests
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
This PR refines AMD mirror gating amidst new MI300 nodes.

## Mirror removals

Mirror blocks removed from the source test files to smoothly transition again to gating signal:

| File | Test Label | Previous AMD Device |
|------|-----------|-----------|
| [engine.yaml](.buildkite/test_areas/engine.yaml) | V1 e2e (2 GPUs) | mi325_2 |
| [engine.yaml](.buildkite/test_areas/engine.yaml) | V1 e2e (4 GPUs) | mi325_4 |
| [misc.yaml](.buildkite/test_areas/misc.yaml) | V1 Spec Decode | mi325_1 |
| [models_basic.yaml](.buildkite/test_areas/models_basic.yaml) | Basic Models Tests (Other) | mi325_1 |
| [models_language.yaml](.buildkite/test_areas/models_language.yaml) | Language Models Test (Extended Generation) | mi325_1 |
| [models_multimodal.yaml](.buildkite/test_areas/models_multimodal.yaml) | Multi-Modal Models (Standard) 4: other + whisper | mi325_1 |

## Mirrors kept

New `mi300_1` (or `mi250_1` where noted) mirrors:

| File | Test Label | From -> To |
|------|-----------|-----------|
| [entrypoints.yaml](.buildkite/test_areas/entrypoints.yaml) | Entrypoints Integration (API Server openai - Part 1) | mi325_1 -> mi300_1 |
| [entrypoints.yaml](.buildkite/test_areas/entrypoints.yaml) | Entrypoints Integration (API Server openai - Part 2) | mi325_1 -> mi300_1 |
| [misc.yaml](.buildkite/test_areas/misc.yaml) | V1 Sample + Logits | mi325_1 -> mi300_1 |
| [misc.yaml](.buildkite/test_areas/misc.yaml) | V1 Core + KV + Metrics | mi325_1 -> mi300_1 |
| [models_language.yaml](.buildkite/test_areas/models_language.yaml) | Language Models Test (Extended Pooling) | mi325_1 -> mi300_1 |
| [models_multimodal.yaml](.buildkite/test_areas/models_multimodal.yaml) | Multi-Modal Models (Standard) 1: qwen2 | mi325_1 -> mi300_1 |
| [models_multimodal.yaml](.buildkite/test_areas/models_multimodal.yaml) | Multi-Modal Models (Standard) 3: llava + qwen2_vl | mi325_1 -> mi300_1 |
| [models_multimodal.yaml](.buildkite/test_areas/models_multimodal.yaml) | Multi-Modal Models (

prs.title: [Quant] Consolidate GPTQ: rename gptq_marlin.py to auto_gptq.py
prs.author: chengyinie
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Consolidate GPTQ quantization by renaming `gptq_marlin.py` to `auto_gptq.py` as requested in #37765.
**Key changes:**
- Renames `gptq_marlin.py` → `auto_gptq.py`
- `GPTQMarlinConfig.get_name()` now returns `"auto_gptq"`
- Adds `override_quantization_method()` to auto-convert models with `quant_method: gptq` to use `auto_gptq`
- Updates all imports across the codebase to use `auto_gptq` module
- Adds `auto_gptq` to overrides list in `model.py`
- Adds `test_auto_gptq.py` for the new quantization method
- Updates `test_gptq_marlin.py` skipif condition to check `auto_gptq` support
- Maintains backward compatibility: `quantization="gptq"` still works (maps to GPTQMarlinConfig)
Closes #37765

## Test Plan
```bash
pytest tests/quantization/test_auto_gptq.py -v -s
pytest tests/quantization/test_gptq_dynamic.py -v -s
pytest tests/quantization/test_lm_head.py -v -s
pytest tests/models/quantization/test_gptq_marlin.py -v -s

## Test Result
 All passed.
---
<details>
<summary> Essential Elements of an Effective PR Description Checklist </summary>

- [*] The purpose of the PR, such as "Fix some issue (link existing issues this PR will resolve)".
- [*] The test plan, such as providing test command.
- [*] The test results, such as pasting the results comparison before and after, or e2e results
- [ ] (Optional) The necessary documentation update, such as updating `supported_models.md` and `examples` for a new model.

</details>

prs.title: [ROCm] Cap Triton paged attention block size to fix ROCm shared memory OOM
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
Hybrid Mamba models (e.g. Jamba) inflate block_size to 2048 to align attention and Mamba page sizes. When the ROCm custom paged attention kernel rejects this (it only supports 16/32), the Triton fallback kernel_paged_attention_2d used 2048 as its tile size, requesting 262144 bytes of shared memory and thus exceeding the MI325X hardware limit of 65536 bytes. Cap TRITON_BLOCK_SIZE at 128. The kernel already decouples tile size from physical block size via l_block_idx/internal_offsets addressing, so this is safe.

### Test plan

- [x] `pytest tests/models/language/generation/test_hybrid.py`

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [ROCm][Engine] Fix GPU memory leaks in engine shutdown and test workaround for async KV prefix cache reset
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
When running in-process (`VLLM_ENABLE_V1_MULTIPROCESSING=0`), shutting down the engine leaked GPU memory because:

- **`supports_kw` LRU cache pinned model weights**: The `@lru_cache` on `supports_kw` was keyed on bound methods (e.g. `model.forward`), which held strong references to the model instance and all its GPU tensors for the lifetime of the cache. Fixed by unwrapping bound methods to `__func__` before caching.
- **`gc.freeze()` was never undone**: `EngineCore.__init__` calls `gc.freeze()` to reduce GC overhead during serving, but `shutdown()` never called `gc.unfreeze()`, making all engine objects invisible to the garbage collector.
- **No explicit GPU resource teardown**: `GPUWorker.shutdown()` never freed model weights, KV caches, the rotary embedding cache (`_ROPE_DICT`), compilation contexts, or the workspace singleton.

### Test workaround for prefix cache reset

`reset_prefix_cache` could return `False` indefinitely when async KV store transfers (CPU offloading) were in flight, because the engine stops stepping after all requests finish and never polls the worker for completed transfers. As a temporary workaround (per reviewer suggestion), `_wait_for_prefix_cache_reset` in the test now sends dummy single-token prefills between retries to force the engine to step, which naturally drains completed transfers and frees GPU blocks.

### UCX fix

Bump UCX branch in ROCm Dockerfile to `33b3b2a2` which fixes a memory leak in UCX when used with NIXL.

## Test plan

- [x] `pytest tests/v1/kv_offload/ tests/v1/kv_connector/unit/ tests/v1/core/test_scheduler.py tests/v1/core/test_prefix_caching.py tests/v1/core/test_reset_prefix_cache_e2e.py tests/v1/simple_kv_offload/test_integration.py -v`

### [LEGACY] Initial Test plan and Motivation

- [x] `pytest tests/v1/kv_offload/test_cpu_offloading.py::test_cpu_offloading[TRITON_ATTN-48]`

Motivation: https://buildkite.com/vllm/amd-ci/builds/7062/steps/canvas?sid=019d382d-caab-452a-bf83-1c611ef498b6

prs.title: [Aiter][ROCm] gdn_linear_attn kernel fusion
prs.author: tpopp
prs.body_head:
With more recent aiter kernels, this combines more kernels together, reducing kernel overhead and optimizing further the computations.

For Qwen3Next, the lm_eval accuracy remains consistent at 0.86 and 0.82 for flexible/strict matching and performance improves by 1-4% depending on batch size.

prs.title: [ROCm] Expanded sparse MLA support
prs.author: ekuznetsov139
prs.body_head:
This PR:
* Fixes a bug in cache_kernels.cu that resulted in incorrect results for block_stride not multiple of 16
* Adds block size 16 to supported sparse MLA block sizes on ROCm
* Fixes a few bugs in the Python code
* Adds optimized Triton kernels with support of arbitrary block size for two of the steps
* In case of rocm_fp8_paged_mqa_logits, routes to the Triton kernel by default, since AITER  is slower for block size 1 and it does not handle block size 16 correctly

I've verified that, after this PR, both GLM-5-FP8 and DeepSeek-V3.2 work correctly on ROCm (MI325  / gfx942) with both --block-size 1 and --block-size 16.

prs.title: [Model][Hardware][AMD][Kernel]: Enable e2e QK Norm + RoPE + KV Cache runtime fusion for Qwen3-30B-A3B on ROCM_ATTN, ROCM_AITER_FA, and ROCM_AITER_UNIFIED_ATTN
prs.author: jhu960213
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

- Enable e2e runtime QK Norm + RoPE + KV Cache fusion for Qwen3-30B. At compile time, we swap the aforementioned fusion pattern & fuse with aiter's high-performance HIP kernel `fused_qk_norm_rope_cache_pts_quant_shuffle ` for all decoder layers that satisfy a preset token compile range guarded by `rope_kvcache_fusion_max_token_num`. 
- This kernel supports writing KV Cache in both shuffle and non-shuffle layouts. ROCM_AITER_FA and ROCM_AITER_UNIFIED_ATTN require the fused kernel to write in non-shuffle mode, as this corresponds to the flash layout expected by FA variants. 
- As for ROCM_ATTN, modifications were needed on both the prefill and decode path kernels (prefill Triton, decode Triton 2D for non-power-of-2 block sizes, and decode paged attention ll4 GFX9 variants) so that they can read the value cache in the interleaved mode, which the fused kernel writes in when shuffle=True. The key cache read logic is untouched as the fused kernel already writes in the correct format, which the prefill and decode paths expect.
- When shuffle=True, the fused kernel writes K with shape (blocks, heads, dim/x, block_size, x) and V with shape (blocks, heads, block_size/x, dim, x). Decode paths already natively read K in the same format, but it currently hardcodes reading of the V cache with an expected shape of (blocks, heads, dims, block_size).

## Test Plan

To test this fusion out, please pass the attached toggle through the pass config under the `--compilation-config` when launching with `vllm serve`:

`'pass_config': {
        'fuse_qk_norm_rope_kvcache': True,
        'rope_kvcache_fusion_max_token_num': 256
}`

For example, something like this:

`VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1 \
vllm serve /path-to-your/Qwen3-30B-A3B \
  --host 0.0.0.0 \
  --port 8080 \
  --attention-backend ROCM_ATTN \
  --no-enable-prefix-caching \
  -O1 \
  --compilation-config '{
    "pass_config": {
      "fuse_qk_norm_rope_kvcache": true,
      "rope_kvcache_fusion_ma

prs.title: [compile] Add FlashInfer FP8 async TP fusion and preserve allreduce fusion ordering #27893
prs.author: baonudesifeizhai
prs.body_head:
## Purpose https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/27893 This PR enables AsyncTP fusion for FlashInfer FP8 GEMMs by rewriting: - `all_gather + vllm.bmm_fp8` - `vllm.bmm_fp8 + reduce_scatter` into SymmetricMemory-backed fused ops using `bmm_fp8_out`. ## Test Plan https://paste.ubuntu.com/p/MKynCPPj5B/ ## Test Result ---

prs.title: [ROCm] Implement GPU-to-NUMA-node detection
prs.author: pschlan-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Implement automatic NUMA topology detection on ROCm platforms via the amdsmi library (which is already a dependency).

## Test Plan
Run vLLM with `--numa-bind`, observe correct topology detection

## Test Result
Topology is detected correctly on the test system with 8 GPUs connected to 2 NUMA nodes:
```INFO 04-16 12:57:41 [numa_utils.py:111] Auto-detected NUMA nodes for GPUs: [0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1]```

prs.title: Make v1 KV cache initialization messages device-neutral
prs.author: whk-lab
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Fix GPU-specific init-time KV cache reporting in `v1/core/kv_cache_utils.py` for shared initialization paths that are also used by non-GPU backends.

The KV cache memory accounting in this path already operates on generic `available_memory`, and CPU workers also feed into the same path via `determine_available_memory()`. However, several logs and errors still hardcode GPU-specific wording such as `GPU KV cache size` and `available GPU memory`, which is misleading on non-GPU backends.

## Test Plan
Ran the following locally:
```bash
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/v1/engine/test_init_error_messaging.py -v
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/v1/core/test_kv_cache_utils.py -k "report_kv_cache_config_uses_device_neutral_messages" -v
.venv/bin/pre-commit run --files vllm/v1/core/kv_cache_utils.py tests/v1/engine/test_init_error_messaging.py tests/v1/core/test_kv_cache_utils.py
```
## Test Result

- tests/v1/engine/test_init_error_messaging.py: 2 passed
- tests/v1/core/test_kv_cache_utils.py -k "report_kv_cache_config_uses_device_neutral_messages": 1 passed
- pre-commit on changed files: passed

On macOS arm64, uv pip install -r requirements/test.in could not be fully resolved because runai-model-streamer==0.15.7 does not provide compatible wheels for this platform, so I used a minimal local environment to run the targeted tests above.




---

prs.title: [ROCm] Add gfx1102/gfx1103 support
prs.author: mgehre-amd
prs.body_head:
gfx1103 (RDNA 3, e.g. Radeon 780M iGPU) was missing from the HIP_SUPPORTED_ARCHS list in CMakeLists.txt and from the compile-time architecture macros in skinny_gemms.cu and attention.cu.

The skinny GEMM and attention kernels defined __HIP__GFX1X__ and __HIP__GFX11__ by listing individual arch targets (__gfx1100__, __gfx1101__, __gfx1150__, __gfx1151__) but missed __gfx1103__. This caused wvSplitK kernel bodies to compile as UNREACHABLE_CODE (assert false) on gfx1103 while the wave32 reduction code was still active, resulting in a crash at runtime.

Replace __HIP__GFX11__ / __HIP__GFX12__ with the compiler-provided __GFX11__ / __GFX12__ umbrella macros which are defined for every target in each family. This avoids needing to enumerate individual targets and automatically covers future gfx11xx/gfx12xx additions.
